'''Suaia Matagi''' (born 23 March 1988), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Sui"''', is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He is both a [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] international.

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]], the [[Sydney Roosters]], the [[Penrith Panthers]] and the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[NRL]], and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has also spent time on dual registration from Castleford at [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Early life==
Matagi was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand, to Samoan parents, the second youngest of six brothers.<ref name=7May2016-FoxSports /> He attended [[Kelston Boys' High School]]<ref name=re-signed /> before dropping out at age 14, having become involved in [[Gangs in New Zealand|gang violence]], and heavy alcohol abuse.<ref name=7May2016-FoxSports>{{cite web|last=Hooper|first=James|date=7 May 2016|title=How three years in prison inspired Penrith prop Suaia Matagi's successful rugby league career|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/how-three-years-in-prison-inspired-penrith-prop-suaia-matagis-successful-rugby-league-career/news-story/167f9e6385fbb6cfcb70f2047aedcceb|work=Fox Sports|access-date=4 October 2016}}</ref><ref name=7Jul2013-Stuff />

In 2006, at the age of 17, Matagi was sentenced to three years in prison for assaulting two boys during a drunken rage that occurred when he was 15.<ref name=12Jan2014-FoxSports>{{cite web|last=Lane|first=Daniel|date=12 January 2014|title=NRL player Suaia Matagi on long, hard road from jail cell|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/nrl-player-suaia-matagi-on-long-hard-road-from-jail-cell-20140111-30nkk.html|publisher=Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=4 October 2016|archive-date=16 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140516224032/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/nrl-player-suaia-matagi-on-long-hard-road-from-jail-cell-20140111-30nkk.html|url-status=live}}</ref> He served his sentence at [[Mount Eden Prison]], and later [[List of correctional facilities in New Zealand#Manawatu Prison|Manawatu Prison]];<ref name=7May2016-FoxSports /> his participation in rehabilitation courses saw him granted [[Parole#New Zealand|parole]] after one year.<ref name="crash course">{{cite web|last=Burgess|first=Michael|date=18 August 2013|title=Matagi's crash course inside|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10913863|publisher=New Zealand Herald|access-date=6 October 2016|archive-date=9 October 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161009111258/http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10913863|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Following his release from prison, Matagi took up rugby league in 2008, having never previously played any organised sport. He played for the [[Te Atatu Roosters]], and later the [[Mount Albert Lions]] in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition. Matagi was selected to play for the [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland representative team]], and the [[New Zealand Residents rugby league team|New Zealand Residents]]<ref name=7Jul2013-Stuff>{{cite web|last=Stanley|first=Ben|date=7 July 2013|title=Matagi's journey: From prison to Mt Smart|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/8888786/Matagis-journey-From-prison-to-Mt-Smart|publisher=stuff.co.nz|access-date=4 October 2016}}</ref> for four consecutive years between 2009 and 2012.<ref name=12Jan2014-FoxSports /><ref>{{cite web|date=19 October 2011|title=NZ Residents Premiers Team 2011 named|url=http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41543|publisher=rleague.com|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111020131634/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41543|archive-date=20 October 2011}}</ref> He was named man of the match in Auckland's 44-34 victory over the [[South Island rugby league team|South Island Scorpions]] in the [[NZRL Men's National Competition|National Competition]]'s 2011 Grand Final.<ref>{{cite web|date=18 October 2011|title=Auckland Pride retain National title|url=http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41530|publisher=rleague.com|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111020153909/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41530|archive-date=20 October 2011}}</ref>

===New Zealand Warriors===
Matagi made his debut for the [[Auckland Vulcans]], the [[New Zealand Warriors]]' feeder team, in the [[New South Wales Cup]] on 3 April 2010. He played regularly for the Vulcans in 2012, and was named 2012 New Zealand domestic player of the year. Matagi trialed with the Warriors during the 2013 pre-season, impressing coach [[Matthew Elliott (rugby league)|Matthew Elliott]] enough to earn an NRL contract.<ref>{{cite web|date=7 February 2013|title=Strong Vulcan flavour in Warriors|url=http://tvnz.co.nz/rugby-league-news/strong-vulcan-flavour-in-warriors-5336072|publisher=Television New Zealand|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130210084805/http://tvnz.co.nz/rugby-league-news/strong-vulcan-flavour-in-warriors-5336072|archive-date=10 February 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Rosser|first=Corey|date=7 May 2013|title=League star on an inspirational journey|url=http://www.waikatoindependent.co.nz/2013/05/league-star-on-an-inspirational-journey/13351/|publisher=Waikato Independent|access-date=11 May 2013|archive-date=25 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150125145943/http://www.waikatoindependent.co.nz/2013/05/league-star-on-an-inspirational-journey/13351/|url-status=live}}</ref>

Matagi made his NRL debut for the Warriors on 11 May 2013 against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] at [[Wellington Regional Stadium|Westpac Stadium]].<ref name=7Jul2013-Stuff /> On 11 June 2013, he re-signed with the Warriors until the end of 2014.<ref name=re-signed>{{cite web|date=11 June 2013|title=Vodafone Warriors re-sign quintet|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2013/06/11/vodafone-warriors-re-sign-quintet.html|publisher=warriors.kiwi|access-date=6 October 2016|archive-date=9 October 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161009103330/http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2013/06/11/vodafone-warriors-re-sign-quintet.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Matagi played 11 games for the Warriors in 2013, scoring a try in their match against the [[Penrith Panthers]] in round 23. On 22 September 2013, Matagi was named at prop in the 2013 [[New South Wales Cup]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|date=22 September 2013|title=2013 NSWRL.com.au VB NSW Cup Team of the Year - Announcement|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=84880&title=2013-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year---announcement|publisher=NSWRL.com.au|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140111155627/http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=84880&title=2013-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year---announcement|archive-date=11 January 2014}}</ref>

Matagi was a member of the Warriors' squad that played in the [[2014 NRL Auckland Nines|2014]] and [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines|2015 Auckland Nines]] pre-season tournaments. On 2 March 2014, Matagi re-signed with the Warriors until the end of 2016.<ref name=2Mar2014-Stuff>{{cite web|last=Long|first=David|date=2 March 2014|title=From prison to the penthouse - Suaia Matagi|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/9779559/From-prison-to-the-penthouse-Suaia-Matagi|publisher=stuff.co.nz|access-date=4 October 2016}}</ref> He played in all 24 of the Warriors' regular season games in 2014, but played in only 1 game during the first half of 2015 before being dropped to the Warriors' New South Wales Cup team. 
[[File:Suaia Matagi.jpg|thumb|right|Matagi playing for the Warriors in 2015]]
In May 2015, Matagi requested, and was granted, a release from his contract with the Warriors in order to sign with the [[Sydney Roosters]] effective immediately.<ref>{{cite web|last=Harvey|first=Sarah|date=15 May 2015|title=Warriors prop Suaia Matagi inks deal with the Sydney Roosters after seeking early release|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/68590783/warriors-prop-suaia-matagi-inks-deal-with-the-sydney-roosters-after-seeking-early-release|publisher=stuff.co.nz|access-date=15 May 2015|archive-date=24 September 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924162417/http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/68590783/warriors-prop-suaia-matagi-inks-deal-with-the-sydney-roosters-after-seeking-early-release|url-status=live}}</ref>

[[Stuff.co.nz]], and ''[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]'' described Matagi as having built a [[cult following]] during his time at the Warriors.<ref name=2Mar2014-Stuff /><ref name=JWH />

===Sydney Roosters===
Upon moving to the Roosters, Matagi played for their feeder club, the [[Wyong Roos]], in the New South Wales Cup. An injury to [[Jared Waerea-Hargreaves]] saw Matagi play seven games for the Sydney Roosters in the back-end of 2015.<ref name=JWH>{{cite web|last=Lane|first=Daniel|date=31 August 2015|title=Roosters prop Jared Waerea-Hargreaves tips Suaia Matagi to strike fear in opposition defenders|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/sydney-roosters/roosters-prop-jared-waereahargreaves-tips-suaia-matagi-to-strike-fear-in-opposition-defenders-20150831-gjbvsp.html|publisher=Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=6 October 2016}}</ref>

===Penrith Panthers===
On 4 November 2015, Matagi signed a one-year contract with the [[Penrith Panthers]], with an option for a second year.<ref>{{cite web|date=4 November 2015|title=Matagi joins the Panthers|url=http://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2015/11/04/matagi_joins_the_pan.html|publisher=penrithpanthers.com.au|access-date=4 November 2015|archive-date=7 November 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151107023126/http://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2015/11/04/matagi_joins_the_pan.html|url-status=live}}</ref> He was a late addition to the Panthers' [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines|2016 Auckland Nines]] squad following an injury to [[Reagan Campbell-Gillard]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Kennedy|first=Chris|date=5 February 2016|title=Surprise call-up sends Panthers' Matagi home|url=http://www.nrl.com/surprise-call-up-sends-panthers-matagi-home/tabid/10874/newsid/92610/default.aspx|publisher=NRL.com}}</ref> Matagi was a regular for the Panthers' in 2016, playing a total of 23 games.

===Parramatta Eels===
In November 2016, Matagi signed a two-year contract with the [[Parramatta Eels]].<ref>{{cite web|date=10 November 2016|title=Eels sign Suaia Matagi|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2016/11/10/eels_sign_suaia_mata.html|publisher=parraeels.com.au|access-date=11 November 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=10 November 2016|title=Parramatta Has Just Announced ANOTHER Signing|url=http://www.triplem.com.au/sydney/sport/nrl/news/2016/11/eels-sign-panthers-suaia-matagi/|publisher=Triple M|access-date=11 November 2016}}</ref>  Matagi was part of the Parramatta side which finished fourth on the table and made the finals for the first time since 2009.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/03/08/matagi-the-eels-new-strike-weapon/|title=Matagi the Eels' new strike weapon|date=8 March 2017|website=National Rugby League|access-date=16 March 2019|archive-date=24 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180624232816/https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/03/08/matagi-the-eels-new-strike-weapon/|url-status=live}}</ref>  In 2018, Matagi started the first two games of the season from the interchange bench.  Following Parramatta's humiliating 54-0 Round 2 up defeat by Manly, Matagi was left out of the Parramatta side by coach Brad Arthur but was recalled 5 weeks later in Round 7 where Parramatta defeated Manly 44-10.  Matagi played a total of nine games for Parramatta in the [[2018 NRL season]] as the club endured a horrid campaign finishing last on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/eels/parramatta-eels-season-2018-review-peter-sterlings-biggest-fear-after-horror-campaign/news-story/14f79c1c637fa8d03bbfa6d6b63b55f1/|title=Peter Sterlings biggest fear after horror campaign|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=2 September 2018|access-date=29 April 2021|archive-date=8 March 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308181038/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/eels/parramatta-eels-season-2018-review-peter-sterlings-biggest-fear-after-horror-campaign/news-story/14f79c1c637fa8d03bbfa6d6b63b55f1|url-status=live}}</ref>

[[File:Suaia Matagi Wentworthville.jpg|thumb|right|Matagi playing for the Wentworthville Magpies in 2018]]
In June 2018, Matagi was selected to play for NSW residents against the QLD residents side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2018/06/24/final-team--intrust-super-nsw-residents/|title=FINAL TEAM - Intrust Super NSW Residents|date=24 June 2018|website=New South Wales Rugby League|access-date=16 March 2019|archive-date=26 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180626165515/https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2018/06/24/final-team--intrust-super-nsw-residents/|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 1 July 2018, Matagi signed a two-year deal to join English side [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] beginning in 2019. Matagi said of the move "I am extremely excited to take my football to the next level under the leadership of coach [[Simon Woolford]] and I'm also looking forward to the challenge of playing in the English Super League, joining a proud club like the Huddersfield Giants is a privilege and I will represent our fans to the best of my ability".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/11422767/huddersfield-giants-sign-samoa-prop-suaia-matagi-from-parramatta|title=Huddersfield Giants sign Samoa prop Suaia Matagi from Parramatta|website=Sky Sports|access-date=16 March 2019|archive-date=2 July 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180702150747/http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/11422767/huddersfield-giants-sign-samoa-prop-suaia-matagi-from-parramatta|url-status=live}}</ref>

==== Castleford Tigers (loan) ====
On 25 November 2020, it was reported that Matagi had signed for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on a season-long loan from Huddersfield.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 Nov 2020|access-date=27 Jan 2021|title=Castleford bring in Suaia Matagi on loan from Huddersfield|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-bring-in-suaia-matagi-on-loan-from-huddersfield/|archive-date=19 January 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210119205217/https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-bring-in-suaia-matagi-on-loan-from-huddersfield/|url-status=live}}</ref> He sustained a calf injury during pre-season training which forced him to miss the start of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=Dave |date=12 March 2021 |title=Castleford Tigers suffer Suaia Matagi blow on eve of new season |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-suffer-suaia-matagi-blow-on-eve-of-new-season-3163144 |access-date=5 November 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He made his Castleford debut on 22 May against the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=22 May 2021 |title=Super League: Gareth Widdop the hat-trick hero in Warrington Wolves' 38-14 win over Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/warrington-vs-castleford/55522 |access-date=5 November 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 10 October 2021, it was announced that Matagi had signed a deal to remain at [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] on a permanent basis, joining the Tigers on a two-year contract.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-10-10 |title=Suaia Matagi: Castleford agree two-year deal with New Zealand prop |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58862739 |access-date=2022-04-11 |website=BBC Sport |archive-date=11 April 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220411024559/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58862739 |url-status=live }}</ref>

Matagi scored his first try for the Tigers against [[Wakefield Trinity]] on 14 April 2022.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Newsum |first=Matt |date=2022-04-14 |title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 4-34 Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61113166 |access-date=2022-04-15 |website=BBC Sport |archive-date=15 April 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220415003203/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61113166 |url-status=live }}</ref> Later in the season, he also scored against [[Toulouse Olympique|Toulouse]] and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].

After a strong start to the 2023 season, Matagi was unavailable during June and July as he returned to [[New Zealand]] due to a bereavement.<ref>{{Cite web |date=20 July 2023 |title=Short-term injury update |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8686 |access-date=5 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers |archive-date=5 November 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231105231050/https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8686 |url-status=live }}</ref> He played a total of 15 matches for Castleford in the 2023 season as the club finished 11th on the table narrowly avoiding relegation.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively/|title=Castleford Tigers ace Alex Mellor delivers brutally honest assessment of 'awful' season; 'We've massively underperformed'|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=22 September 2023 }}</ref> On 19 September, Castleford confirmed that Matagi would depart the club upon the expiration of his contract.<ref>{{Cite web |date=19 September 2023 |title=Matagi to depart the Fords |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8764 |access-date=5 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC===
On 4 November 2023, it was announced that Matagi would join [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] for 2024.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=4 Nov 2023|title=Championship move confirmed for former NRL ace following Castleford Tigers departure: 'I'm just getting started'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/championship-move-confirmed-for-former-nrl-ace-following-castleford-tigers-departure-im-just-getting-started|access-date=4 November 2023|archive-date=4 November 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231104103037/https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/championship-move-confirmed-for-former-nrl-ace-following-castleford-tigers-departure-im-just-getting-started|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Matagi made his international debut for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]], playing in all four of their matches, scoring tries against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]]. In May 2014, Matagi played for Samoa in the [[Pacific Rugby League International#2014 test|2014 Pacific Rugby League test]] against [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] to qualify for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]].

In October 2014, Matagi was named in both the New Zealand and Samoan Four Nations squads.<ref>{{cite news|last=Beswick|first=Angela|date=7 October 2014|title=Four Nations: Matagi selected by NZ, Samoa|url=http://www.newshub.co.nz/sport/four-nations-matagi-selected-by-nz-samoa-2014100721|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161009131439/http://www.newshub.co.nz/sport/four-nations-matagi-selected-by-nz-samoa-2014100721|url-status=dead|archive-date=9 October 2016|newspaper=Newshub|access-date=6 October 2016}}</ref> He opted to represent New Zealand,<ref>{{cite web|last=Skipwith|first=David|date=10 October 2014|title=Matagi chooses Kiwis over Samoa|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=11340467|publisher=New Zealand Herald|access-date=6 October 2016}}</ref> playing in one of their four matches, against Samoa.

Matagi was named in New Zealand's train-on squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]],<ref>{{cite web|date=21 September 2016|title=Kiwis add eight to train-on squad|url=http://www.nrl.com/kiwis-add-eight-to-train-on-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/101571/default.aspx|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=6 October 2016|archive-date=5 October 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161005064921/http://www.nrl.com/kiwis-add-eight-to-train-on-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/101571/default.aspx|url-status=live}}</ref> but was not included in the final team.<ref>{{cite web|last=Risso|first=Angelo|date=4 October 2016|title=Kiwis stick with Bromwich as skipper|url=http://www.nrl.com/rapana-heads-nz-test-newcomers/tabid/10874/newsid/101930/default.aspx|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=6 October 2016|archive-date=5 October 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161005111154/http://www.nrl.com/rapana-heads-nz-test-newcomers/tabid/10874/newsid/101930/default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref>

On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]], he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] in their 26-24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England’s [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.

==Personal life==
Matagi is a [[Christians|Christian]].<ref name=12Jan2014-FoxSports /><ref name="crash course" /> He is a father of four children with his partner Fai.<ref name=7May2016-FoxSports />
